text.title: 100k Token Budget and Dependency Tree
text.summary: How the 100k token budget is allocated across task phases and why the dependency tree is the most critical section for executor agent consistency.

rule.statement: Keep every task within 100k total token budget: SDD docs 15% (~15k), dependency tree 10% (~10k), research 25% (~25k), implementation 40% (~40k), testing 10% (~10k).
rule.rationale: Claude's effective code generation quality degrades above ~80k context tokens. The 100k budget with component-level breakdown prevents executor agents from loading too much context before starting to code, which produces truncated or incoherent implementations.

list.item: SDD Docs (15%): constitution.md relevant sections + spec FR-X + design AD-X + contracts.md if API
Dependency Tree (10%): completed task summaries + key code snippets from prior tasks
Research (25%): reading similar existing code patterns in the codebase
Implementation (40%): actual coding — the primary value-producing phase
Testing (10%): writing and running tests, verification

rule.statement: Include a dependency tree in every task that builds on completed prior tasks; without it, agents re-discover patterns, produce inconsistent implementations, and contradict earlier decisions.
rule.rationale: The dependency tree is the mechanism by which patterns established in TASK-001 propagate to TASK-005. Without it, the executor in TASK-005 invents its own approach, breaking consistency with existing code. With it, 30-40% of context budget is freed because research is replaced by targeted pattern reuse.

list.item: Without dependency tree: agent re-discovers patterns (wastes research budget), inconsistent code style, may contradict earlier decisions, duplicates work
With dependency tree: reuses established patterns, consistent code, builds on prior decisions, context focused on implementation
Summary: one sentence — what was done in the dependency task
Files: exact paths created or modified
Patterns: reusable conventions discovered (BaseModel inheritance, email validator, etc.)
Key code: critical code snippet (class definition, key function) — anchors executor's style
Decisions: implementation decisions made (e.g., UUID for PK instead of auto-increment)

rule.statement: Keep each task's "Files to Change" table to 8 or fewer files; if more, split the task.
rule.rationale: More than 8 files means the task is doing too many things. Beyond 8 files, the token budget is consumed by file-reading context, leaving insufficient budget for implementation. Split on logical boundaries: one task per layer (model, service, handler, tests).

text.reference: Estimate token budget for task context components before writing the task
